title: US and Iran sign 14-point memorandum to reduce tensions
summary: The agreement includes lifting the naval blockade and sanctions in exchange for nuclear limitations, generating criticism in both countries.

title: Yen falls to 161 per dollar, its lowest level since July 2024
summary: The Japanese currency depreciates to 161 yen per dollar amid expectations of a Fed rate hike, fueling intervention by the Bank of Japan.

title: Takefusa Kubo will not play against Tunisia due to left knee injury
summary: The Japan national team forward misses the second World Cup match and focuses on recovery at the base camp.

title: Nikkei closes above 70,000 points for the first time
summary: The Tokyo stock index reaches a new record driven by market optimism and gains in the technology sector.

title: DAZN suspends new subscriptions to unlimited football plan due to fee confusion
summary: The platform offers refunds to those who wish to cancel after criticism over lack of clarity in pricing.

title: Democratic People's Party willing to cooperate with LDP government
summary: Opposition party leader Tamaki suggests cooperation while Prime Minister Takaichi hints at a possible coalition.

title: Sentenced to 2 years and 6 months in prison for hitting and killing a pregnant woman
summary: The court considers the responsibility serious due to the harm caused to the fetus, and the sentence is received with relief by the family.

title: Israel breaks contact with EU foreign policy chief
summary: The Israeli government accuses the EU high representative of maintaining an unfair and biased stance against Israel.

title: PayPay requires identity verification to receive QR payments as anti-fraud measure
summary: The app implements mandatory confirmation to prevent scams via fraudulent emails.

title: Five people indicted for corruption in Yatsushiro city hall
summary: The accused face bribery and money laundering charges in a scandal involving council members and former officials.

title: Waves from 2011 earthquake reflected inside Earth and moved Japan 5 mm
summary: Researchers discover that seismic waves from the great east Japan earthquake reflected in deep layers, causing a displacement of the archipelago.

title: ASEAN and Russia agree to strengthen energy cooperation at Kazan summit
summary: Southeast Asian leaders and Russia adopt a joint statement without condemning the Russian invasion of Ukraine, prioritizing energy security.

title: China and North Korea reject G7 statement on denuclearization
summary: Beijing accuses G7 of fueling confrontation, while Pyongyang calls the declaration 'interference' and 'provocation'.

title: Possible nomination of Kevin Warsh as new Fed chairman raises expectations of restrictive policy
summary: The market reacts to Warsh's conservative stance, as the Fed simplifies its statements and moves closer to a rate hike.

title: Massive Ukrainian drone attack sets fire to one of Russia's largest refineries
summary: The attack, the largest against Moscow, causes a fire at a key refinery and raises tensions in the conflict.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi's cabinet approval falls, worrying the ruling party
summary: The decline in popular support causes concern among LDP lawmakers about the political future.

title: Liberal Democratic Party begins debate on anti-espionage law
summary: The initiative seeks to strengthen national security against potential foreign intelligence threats, according to party sources.

title: Japanese manga exports could reach 1 trillion yen, according to government and private sector
summary: The government and industry explore creating a national platform to boost overseas manga sales.

title: Emperor Naruhito views Vermeer's 'Girl with a Pearl Earring' during visit to the Netherlands
summary: The Japanese monarch visits the Mauritshuis museum and admires the masterpiece, highlighting cultural exchange between both countries.

title: US government intervenes to block Nippon Steel's acquisition of US Steel
summary: The US administration pressures to avoid plant closures, while the steel union demands labor guarantees from the Japanese company.
